title: Greek Roast Lamb
subtitle: Slow-roasted leg of lamb with lemon, garlic, and oregano
excerpt: Greek roast lamb is a whole leg studded with garlic and rubbed with lemon, dried oregano, and olive oil, then slow-roasted until it falls from the bone. It is the Easter dish across Greece, cooked for hours at a low temperature. The result is deeply flavoured, falling-tender meat.
sourceNotes: Roast leg of lamb at Easter is the central dish of Greek celebration cooking. The slow, low method common in Greek homes produces a leg that requires no carving; the meat pulls away from the bone. Lemon, garlic, and dried oregano are the defining flavours.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Rub the lamb with the marinade the night before and refrigerate, covered.

content.text: Greek roast lamb is a whole leg cooked low and slow, studded with garlic and coated in lemon, olive oil, and oregano. After 3 hours to 3 hours 30 minutes at 160°C the meat is very tender and pulls away from the bone without carving. It is a simple method that needs little active attention.
Potatoes and tomatoes added to the tin roast in the lamb juices and come out sweet and well-seasoned. No separate side dish is needed.

Make small deep cuts all over the lamb using a sharp knife. Push a sliver of garlic into each cut. Mix the lemon zest and juice, olive oil, oregano, rosemary leaves, salt, and pepper into a paste. Rub this all over the lamb. Leave at room temperature for 30 minutes, or refrigerate overnight.
Heat the oven to 200°C. Place the lamb in a large roasting tin. Scatter the potato chunks and tomatoes around it. Pour in {{chicken-stock}} of stock. Cover the tin tightly with foil.
Reduce the oven to 160°C and roast for 3 hours covered. Remove the foil and roast for a further 30 minutes until the lamb is deep brown and the potatoes are golden.
Rest for 30 minutes before serving. The meat should pull away from the bone easily. Spoon the cooking juices over the potatoes and lamb to serve.

items.symptom: The lamb is not tender enough to pull
items.cause: Not enough time at low temperature
items.fix: Replace the foil and return to the oven for another 30 to 45 minutes. The leg can stay in the oven at 150°C almost indefinitely without drying out if covered with foil.

items.symptom: The potatoes are not cooked through
items.cause: Cut too large, or placed too far from the lamb juices
items.fix: Cut potatoes into chunks no larger than 5 cm. Tuck them under and around the lamb so they catch the juices.

items.cause: Not enough liquid added at the start
items.fix: Add a splash of hot water or stock and re-cover. Check occasionally during the covered roasting period.

content.text: Roast lamb at Easter is the centrepiece of Greek family life. The whole day is built around it: the morning church service, the early afternoon lighting of the coals or setting of the oven, the long communal lunch. The dish needs time but not skill, and the result feeds a crowd without any effort at the table.
